slides.title: The Final Word 🧹
slides.content:
There is a third block you can attach to the end of a `try / catch`. It is called **`finally`**.

Code placed inside `finally { ... }` is strictly guaranteed to run, no matter what happens.

slides.title: Seeing Guarantee in Action 👀
slides.content:
If the try block succeeds: Finally runs.
If the try block explodes: Catch runs, then Finally runs.
If a RETURN statement happens: Finally runs first before letting the return happen!

It is an absolute guarantee.

quizData.question: When does the `finally` block execute?

options.text: Only if there are NO errors.
It always executes, regardless of whether the `try` block succeeded or crashed.

quizData.explanation: The finally block is unconditionally guaranteed to run.

slides.title: Why do we need it? 🔓
slides.content:
You might ask: "Why not just write code below the Try/Catch entirely?"

Usually, `finally` is used to **close** important external things. For example, if you open a connection to the User's Database, and it explodes, you MUST close the connection, or you will cause a memory leak.

The `finally` block guarantees your `database.close()` command runs, preventing permanent damage to the server.
